h1: Your Puppy's First Weeks Home: What to Expect

entity_definitions.definition: A new puppy's first month runs on routine: fixed feeding times, a consistent toilet spot, a playpen it owns, calm sleep habits, and gentle socialisation inside the 3-to-16-week window, with vaccinations at weeks 6, 8, and 12 and homecoming around week 9.

content_outline.heading: Your Puppy's First Weeks Home: What to Expect
What does a puppy's first week look like?

internal_links.anchor: what the free training lesson covers

faq.question: How long until a puppy settles in?
faq.answer_direction: Most settle visibly in 3 to 7 days with a fixed routine; full confidence takes 3 to 4 weeks.

faq.question: Is night whimpering normal?
faq.answer_direction: Yes, for the first few nights; comfort toy + consistent calm response shortens it. It is adjustment, not distress.

faq.question: When is the first vet visit?
faq.answer_direction: Your puppy arrives vaccinated to schedule (weeks 6/8 done, home ~week 9); week 12 completes the core course at the vet.

faq.question: How often do puppies eat?
faq.answer_direction: Three fixed meals a day at this age, same food it came with; transition brands gradually if at all.

faq.question: When can the puppy go outside?
faq.answer_direction: Carried exposure immediately (socialisation window!); ground walks in busy areas after the week-12 vaccination per your vet.

faq.question: What if something feels wrong in week one?
faq.answer_direction: WhatsApp us first, any time; if a puppy from us falls sick in the first 5 days, bring it back and we nurse it.
